data.name: AddRangeToCollection_Description
data.value: Adds a set of items to the specified collection.

data.name: AddRangeToCollection_Items_Description
data.value: The items to be added to the collection.

data.name: WaitFile_PollingIntervalSeconds_Description
data.value: Specifies the amount of time in seconds for the file re-check. Any value less than 0.5 will be clamped to 0.5. Make sure to keep this value lesser than Timeout value.

data.name: Common_TimeoutSeconds_Description
data.value: Specifies the amount of time in seconds to wait for the activity to run before an error is thrown. The default value is 30s.

data.name: WaitFile_Description
data.value: Waits until the file be available.

data.name: WaitFile_Result_Description
data.value: The FileInfo object of the respective file when found.

data.name: Common_ContinueOnError_Description
data.value: Specifies to continue executing the remaining activities even if the current activity failed. Only boolean values are allowed.

data.name: Common_SearchPattern_Description
data.value: The search string defines file name patterns in the path. It supports literal text and wildcards (*, ?, !, |), but not regular expressions. Use ! for exclusions (e.g., !*.txt), and | for multiple patterns (e.g., *.png|*.gif|*.jpeg). Default is *.* (all files).

data.name: WaitFile_DynamicFile_Description
data.value: When true, the behavior of the activity changes to monitor a specific folder until find a file that corresponds to the search pattern informed.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_DeleteEmptyFolders_Description
data.value: Determines if the left empty folders after files deletion must also be deleted.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_Description
data.value: Deletes all files and folders from a specified folder.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_Folder_Description
data.value: The folder path to be cleaned up.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_LastWriteTime_Description
data.value: Deletes only the files with last write time till this reference date. Default is DateTime.Now.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_FilesDeleted_Description
data.value: The number of files that were deleted.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_SearchOption_Description
data.value: Specifies whether the search operation should include only the current directory or should include all subdirectories.

data.name: CleanUpFolder_FoldersDeleted_Description
data.value: The number of folders that were deleted.

data.name: Zip_Description
data.value: Compresses files and folders into a zip archive.

data.name: Zip_ToCompress_Description
data.value: Collection of file and directory paths to compress.

data.name: Zip_TextEncoding_Description
data.value: Encoding used for entry names inside the zip archive.

data.name: Zip_CompressionLevel_Description
data.value: Compression level used when creating the archive.

data.name: Zip_CompressedFile_Description
data.value: Index or identifier of the compressed file created (when applicable).

data.name: Zip_FullEntryNames_Description
data.value: Use full entry names (absolute paths) instead of relative paths.

data.name: Zip_FilesCount_Description
data.value: The number of files added to the archive.

data.name: Zip_ZipFilePath_Description
data.value: Path where the zip archive will be created.

data.name: Common_ErrorMsg_ValueNotSupplied
data.value: Value for a required activity argument '{0}' was not supplied.

data.name: Unzip_Description
data.value: Extracts files and folders from a zip archive.

data.name: Unzip_ZipFilePath_Description
data.value: The path to the zip archive to extract.

data.name: Unzip_ExtractTo_Description
data.value: The folder where the archive contents will be extracted.

data.name: Unzip_Overwrite_Description
data.value: If true, existing files will be overwritten during extraction.

data.name: Unzip_ErrorMsg_OutsideDir
data.value: Archive entry would extract outside the target directory.

data.name: Unzip_ErrorMsg_DirNameWithData
data.value: Directory entry in the archive contains file data, which is invalid.

data.name: RandomString_Description
data.value: Generates a random string based on a format pattern.

data.name: RandomString_Format_Description
data.value: Pattern that defines the generated string. Placeholders: 'a' lowercase, 'A' uppercase, '0' digit, '*' any alphanumeric, '?' custom characters, '\\' escape.

data.name: RandomString_Custom_Description
data.value: Additional characters used when the '?' placeholder is present.

data.name: Increment_Description
data.value: Increments a numeric variable by a specified value.

data.name: Increment_Value_Description
data.value: Amount to add to the variable. Must be greater than or equal to 1.

data.name: Decrement_Description
data.value: Decrements a numeric variable by a specified value.

data.name: Decrement_Value_Description
data.value: Amount to subtract from the variable. Must be greater than or equal to 1.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Description
data.value: Replaces the tokens of a string by the values of a Dictionary. Tokens are strings written in a specific pattern, usually enclosed in special characters. Use the combination of 'Pattern' and 'Placeholder' properties to define your token format.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Dictionary_Description
data.value: The dictionary where each key/value pair are used to replace the tokens.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Pattern_Description
data.value: The token text pattern. It can have characters either before and after the placeholder.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Placeholder_Description
data.value: The character used as the placeholder inside the token pattern.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Result_Description
data.value: The resulting text after all tokens have been replaced.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_Content_Description
data.value: The input text where tokens will be replaced.

data.name: ReplaceTokens_CaseSensitive_Description
data.value: Determines if the searching for tokens are case sensitive. The Pattern is also affected.

data.name: NetworkDrive_ErrorMsg_InvalidDriveLetter
data.value: The drive letter input is invalid. Valid inputs are 'X', 'X:' or 'X:\\', where X can be any letter.

data.name: MapDrive_Description
data.value: Maps a Network Drive into the system.

data.name: MapDrive_DriveLetter_Description
data.value: Specifies the drive letter to be mapped. If not provided, a random available letter will be assigned. The expected format is <letter><colon>, for example: "X:", "y:", "Z:".

data.name: MapDrive_Force_Description
data.value: Tries to force the connection if the drive letter is already being in use.

data.name: MapDrive_MappedDrive_Description
data.value: The mapped drive letter if the mapping was successful, it is represented in the format "<letter>:".

data.name: MapDrive_ResponseCode_Description
data.value: Represents the return code of the drive mapping operation. This value indicates whether the operation succeeded or failed, according to the standard Windows error codes.

data.name: MapDrive_ResponseMessage_Description
data.value: Provides the textual description corresponding to the response code. This message explains the result of the operation in a human‑readable format.

data.name: MapDrive_Result_Description
data.value: Returns true if the drive was successfully mapped, false otherwise.

data.name: MapDrive_SharedDrivePath_Description
data.value: The shared drive path to connect with.

data.name: UnmapDrive_DriveLetter_Description
data.value: Specifies the drive letter of the mapped drive to be unmapped. The required format is <letter><colon>, for example: "X:", "y:", "Z:".

data.name: UnmapDrive_ResponseCode_Description
data.value: Represents the return code of the drive mapping operation. This value indicates whether the operation succeeded or failed, according to the standard Windows error codes.

data.name: UnmapDrive_ResponseMessage_Description
data.value: Provides the textual description corresponding to the response code. This message explains the result of the operation in a human‑readable format.

data.name: UnmapDrive_Result_Description
data.value: Returns true if the drive was successfully unmapped, false otherise.

data.name: MapDrive_Credential_Placeholder
data.value: If not provided, current user credentials will be used.

data.name: CultureScope_ErrorMsg_CultureNameNullOrEmpty
data.value: The culture name cannot be null or empty.

data.name: CultureScope_Culture_Description
data.value: The desired culture, eg.: en-US, pt-BR, jp-JP, etc.

data.name: CultureScope_Description
data.value: A container that allows you to run a set of activities over a different culture.

data.name: TerminateProcess_Description
data.value: Tries to gracefully close all instances of the applications corresponding to the specified processes. If not possible, it kills the process for the current user session.

data.name: TerminateProcess_ProcessNames_Description
data.value: The list of names of the processes to be terminated.

data.name: IfActivity_Description
data.value: Evaluates the result of a boolean-returning activity and executes the associated code block if the condition is met.

data.name: IfActivity_ErrorMsg_ConditionReturnsBoolean
data.value: The Condition field requires activities that returns a boolean value.

data.name: Exit_Condition_Description
data.value: Condition which determines if the activity will be evaluated. If empty it assumes True.

data.name: Exit_Description
data.value: Exits from the Container or Time Loop activities interrupting any child executions after it.

data.name: Exit_ErrorMsg_InvalidParent
data.value: Exit activity must be placed inside a Container or Time Loop activities.

data.name: TimeLoop_Description
data.value: Repeats the body activities until the timeout expires, with optional iteration intervals and index tracking. Returns true if the loop ended due to timeout.

data.name: TimeLoop_Timeout_Description
data.value: Defines how long the loop runs. The timeout is checked after each iteration, and ongoing iterations are not interrupted.

data.name: TimeLoop_IntervalSeconds_Description
data.value: The interval in seconds to wait before the next loop.

data.name: TimeLoop_IterationIndex_Description
data.value: The current iteration (zero-based) that is being processed.

data.name: Container_Description
data.value: Its a wrapper that when combined with Exit Activity, interrupts the children execution flow exiting the Container beforehand.

data.name: CheckPoint_Data_Description
data.value: A collection of key/value pairs that provide additional user-defined information about the exception.

data.name: CheckPoint_Description
data.value: Checks if a given expression is true, if is not, thrown the specified exception.

data.name: CheckPoint_Exception_Description
data.value: The exception to throw if the expression is not true.

data.name: PromoteHeaders_AutoRename_Description
data.value: When true, adds a numeric suffix if the column name is already present in the Data Table.

data.name: PromoteHeaders_Description
data.value: Promotes the first row of values to new column headers.

data.name: PromoteHeaders_ErrorMsg_NoData
data.value: There is no rows in the input data table to promote.

data.name: PromoteHeaders_DataTable_Description
data.value: The data table which first row will be promoted to headers.

data.name: DataTableToText_DateTimeFormat_Description
data.value: The date format to be used on the string representation for DateTime types.

data.name: DataTableToText_Description
data.value: Returns a string representation of a datatable on the specified text format (HTML, JSON or XML).

data.name: DataTableToText_Result_Description
data.value: The string representation of the DataTable.

data.name: RemoveEmptyRows_ColumnNames_Description
data.value: The column names to be evaluated.

data.name: RemoveEmptyRows_Description
data.value: Removes the empty rows from a Data Table.

data.name: RemoveEmptyRows_DataTable_Description
data.value: The Data Table whose empty rows will be removed.

data.name: RemoveEmptyRows_MatchingMode_Description
data.value: Determines how a row is evaluated for removal: All — the row is removed if all specified columns are empty. Any — the row is removed if any specified column is empty. If no columns are specified, all columns of the Data Table are considered.

data.name: RemoveEmptyRows_ColumnIndexes_Description
data.value: The column indexes to be evaluated.

data.name: RemoveDataColumns_ColumnsNames_Description
data.value: The column names to be removed.

data.name: RemoveDataColumns_Description
data.value: Removes the specified columns from a Data Table

data.name: RemoveDataColumns_DataTable_Description
data.value: The Data Table from which the columns is to be removed.

data.name: RemoveDataColumns_ColumnIndexes_Description
data.value: The column indexes to be removed.

data.name: TransposeData_Description
data.value: Transposes a DataTable by swapping its rows and columns so that rows become columns and columns become rows.

data.name: TransposeData_DataTable_Description
data.value: The Data Table to be transposed.

data.name: ParseData_Description
data.value: Parses JSON or YAML input into a DataNode for simplified access and manipulation.

data.name: ParseData_Content_Description
data.value: The JSON or YAML text to be parsed into a DataNode.

data.name: ParseData_Culture_Description
data.value: Defines the culture information applied during value conversions (such as int, long, double, or decimal) within the DataNode.

data.name: ParseData_Result_Description
data.value: The resulting DataNode created from the parsed JSON or YAML content.

data.name: LoadDataFile_Description
data.value: Parses a JSON or YAML file into a DataNode for simplified access and manipulation.

data.name: LoadDataFile_Encoding_Description
data.value: Specifies the text encoding used when reading the file. Defaults to UTF-8.

data.name: LoadDataFile_Culture_Description
data.value: Specifies the culture used when converting node values to numeric types (e.g., int, long, double, decimal).

data.name: LoadDataFile_Result_Description
data.value: The DataNode generated from the parsed file content.

data.name: LoadDataFile_FilePath_Description
data.value: The path to the JSON or YAML file to be loaded.

data.name: UnmapDrive_Description
data.value: Removes a mapped network drive from the system.

data.name: MergeData_Description
data.value: Merges the source DataNode into the destination one. Scalar and Sequence values from source overwrite existing ones in destination. Map values are merged recursively.

data.name: MergeData_Source_Description
data.value: The DataNode to merge into the destination instance.

data.name: MergeData_Destination_Description
data.value: The DataNode to be updated with source instance.

data.name: Zip_EntryStructure_Description
data.value: Defines how files are stored inside the ZIP archive: keep relative paths, flatten to root, rename duplicates, or preserve full directory structure. Auto mode flattens when possible and falls back to relative paths if duplicate names are detected.
